questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A physical quantity C is defined by $C = \dfrac{p\,q^{2}}{r^{3}\sqrt{s}}$. If the percentage errors in measuring p, q, r and s are 1%, 2%, 3% and 2% respectively, find the maximum percentage error in C.
questions.explanation: Fractional errors add with their powers: 1(1%) + 2(2%) + 3(3%) + (1/2)(2%) = 1 + 4 + 9 + 1 = 15%.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: With $\mu_0$ and $\varepsilon_0$ the permeability and permittivity of free space, the dimensional formula of $\dfrac{1}{\mu_0\varepsilon_0}$ is:
questions.explanation: $1/(\mu_0\varepsilon_0) = c^{2}$, the square of a speed, so its dimensions are $L^{2}T^{-2}$.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: $\varepsilon_0$ is the permittivity of free space and $\Phi_E$ is the electric flux through a closed surface. The quantity $\varepsilon_0\,\dfrac{d\Phi_E}{dt}$ has the same dimensions as:
questions.explanation: This is the displacement-current term, which has the dimensions of electric current.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: Consider two statements. Statement I: Planck's constant and angular momentum have the same dimensions. Statement II: In Bohr's model the electron's angular momentum is an integral multiple of Planck's constant. Choose the correct option.
questions.optionA: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
questions.optionB: Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
questions.optionC: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
questions.optionD: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
questions.explanation: Planck's constant has dimensions $ML^{2}T^{-1}$, the same as angular momentum, so I is correct. Bohr quantization is $L = n\,h/2\pi$ (a multiple of $h/2\pi$, not of $h$), so II is incorrect.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: The quantity $\sqrt{\dfrac{\mu_0}{\varepsilon_0}}$, where $\mu_0$ is the vacuum permeability and $\varepsilon_0$ the vacuum permittivity, has the same dimensions as:
questions.explanation: $\sqrt{\mu_0/\varepsilon_0}$ is the impedance of free space (about 377 ohm), so it has the dimensions of resistance.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A helicopter flying horizontally at 360 km/h at an altitude of 2 km releases an object, which lands 20 s later. The displacement of the landing point from the point of release is (take g = 10 m/s^2 and neglect air resistance):
questions.explanation: Horizontal range = 100 m/s x 20 s = 2 km; vertical fall = 2 km. Displacement = $\sqrt{2^{2}+2^{2}} = 2\sqrt{2}$ km.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A body of mass 2 kg moving with velocity $\vec{v} = 3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j}$ m/s enters a region of constant force 6 N directed along the +z axis. After remaining in the region for 5/3 s, its velocity is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration along z = F/m = 6/2 = 3 m/s^2; $v_z = a t = 3(5/3) = 5$. The x and y components are unchanged, giving $3\hat{i}+4\hat{j}+5\hat{k}$.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A particle moves along the x-axis with displacement $x = c_0(t^{2} - 2) + c(t - 2)^{2}$, where $c_0$ and c are constants. Which of the following is correct?
questions.optionA: The acceleration of the particle is $2c_0$
questions.optionB: The acceleration of the particle is $2c$
questions.optionC: The initial velocity of the particle is $4c$
questions.optionD: The acceleration of the particle is $2(c + c_0)$
questions.explanation: Differentiating twice: $a = d^{2}x/dt^{2} = 2c_0 + 2c = 2(c + c_0)$, a constant.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: Two projectiles are fired from the same point with equal speeds at angles $(45^{\circ} + \alpha)$ and $(45^{\circ} - \alpha)$ to the horizontal. The ratio of their times of flight is:
questions.explanation: Time of flight is proportional to $\sin\theta$, so the ratio is $\dfrac{\sin(45^{\circ}+\alpha)}{\sin(45^{\circ}-\alpha)} = \dfrac{\cos\alpha+\sin\alpha}{\cos\alpha-\sin\alpha} = \dfrac{1+\tan\alpha}{1-\tan\alpha}$.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: An object of mass 500 g moves along the x-axis with speed $v = 4\sqrt{x}$ m/s. The magnitude of the force acting on it is:
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2025 (April) Question Paper, NTA
